blurb: An explainer breaking down what "solidarity economy" actually means — an approach to economic life built on cooperation, mutualism, and shared ownership rather than competition and extraction.

The hub site of the U.S. Solidarity Economy Network, connecting cooperatives, community land trusts, and mutual-aid initiatives working to build an economy rooted in solidarity rather than extraction.

An essay from the Malcolm X Grassroots Movement's Free the Land project framing solidarity economics as a vehicle for Black self-determination — building cooperative, community-controlled alternatives to extractive capitalism.
ambiguous_note: Title alone confirms the source organization and general topic; the specific framing around self-determination and cooperative ownership is inferred from context rather than stated directly in the title.

blurb: An essay for organizers examining the fraught relationship between social movements and philanthropic funding — asking whether transformative, revolutionary work can survive being financially dependent on the institutions it seeks to change.
ambiguous_note: Title (echoing the well-known 'Revolution Will Not Be Funded' critique) strongly signals the topic, but the exact argument and examples are inferred rather than confirmed from the title alone.

blurb: An overview of Kate Raworth's Doughnut Economics framework from the Doughnut Economics Action Lab — a model for an economy that meets everyone's needs within the ecological limits of the planet.
Dasra's 2024 report mapping the state and trends of philanthropic giving in India, tracking where funding is flowing and where major gaps in social investment remain.
A Monitor Mag piece exploring what a "well-being economy" would look like in practice — reorienting economic policy around human and ecological flourishing rather than GDP growth alone.
ambiguous_note: Title is a vague 'insights into' framing; the specific policy argument and scope are inferred from the well-being-economy topic area rather than stated explicitly.

title_clean: Economic Inequality Seen as Major Challenge Around the World
blurb: A 2025 Pew Research Center global survey finding that majorities across dozens of countries see economic inequality as a major challenge facing their nation, with sharp differences in how urgently people want their governments to act.

title_clean: Views of Government's Role in Aiding the Poor, Health Care, and Social Security
blurb: A Pew Research Center survey tracking how Americans' views on the government's responsibility to help the poor, provide health coverage, and fund Social Security have shifted — and where deep partisan divides remain.
